vrt.dialogDescription: Combine multiple raster datasets into a single virtual layer.
vrt.mosaicHelp: Combines multiple rasters into a single continuous layer. Sources are spatially arranged side-by-side. All sources must share the same CRS, data type, and band count.
vrt.bandStackHelp: Stacks multiple single-band rasters into a multi-band dataset. Each source becomes one band. Sources must have identical grid dimensions and resolution.
vrt.resFinestHelp: Uses the highest resolution (smallest pixel size) among all sources. Other sources are resampled to match.
vrt.resCoarsestHelp: Uses the lowest resolution (largest pixel size). Faster but loses detail from finer sources.
vrt.resAverageHelp: Uses the mean resolution across all sources. Balances detail and performance.
vrt.gridMismatch: Grid dimensions do not match (width, height, or resolution differs)

upload.hintCrs: Ensure the file has a valid coordinate reference system (CRS/SRID)
upload.hintEncoding: Try saving the file with UTF-8 encoding
upload.hintGeometry: Check that the file contains valid geometry data
upload.hintEmpty: The file appears to be empty or contains no geographic features

reservedRename.title: Reserved column names renamed
reservedRename.description: Source columns that clash with GeoLens-internal column names were automatically renamed with a `src_` prefix so both the source data and pipeline geometry can coexist.

dbfTruncation.title: Shapefile field name collisions
dbfTruncation.description: Shapefile DBF field names are capped at 10 characters. Two or more source fields collapsed to the same truncated name and may have been merged during import.

archiveFailed.title: Original file archive failed
archiveFailed.description: The dataset was imported successfully, but copying the original source file to durable storage failed. The dataset is still queryable; re-upload if you need the original file restored.

temporalParseErrors.title: Temporal fields dropped
temporalParseErrors.description: The following temporal values could not be parsed as ISO-8601 dates and were dropped. Re-enter them in the dataset metadata editor to restore them.

dropzone.instructions: Drag and drop files here, or click to browse
dropzone.unsupportedType: This file type is not supported

stac.label: STAC API URL — paste the catalog root endpoint

serviceUrl.label: Service URL
serviceUrl.placeholder: https://example.com/wfs, ArcGIS FeatureServer, or OGC API endpoint
serviceUrl.helpText: Paste a WFS, ArcGIS Feature Service, or OGC API Features URL
serviceUrl.tokenPlaceholder: ArcGIS token, API key, or WFS access token
serviceUrl.tokenHelpText: For ArcGIS Online: create an API key at developers.arcgis.com, or generate a short-lived token at your-org.maps.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/generateToken. For WFS, use your provider's access token. Tokens are not stored.

register.helpText: Catalog existing PostGIS tables from your database as datasets in GeoLens. Use this when you have geographic data already in the database that wasn't imported through GeoLens. Selected tables will be added to the dataset catalog with automatic metadata detection.
register.emptyState: No unregistered tables found in the database.
register.visibilityHelp: Public = visible to all. Restricted = logged-in users only. Private = editors and admins only.

layerPicker.title: Select Layer
layerPicker.emptyState: No layers found in this service

metadata.title: Import Settings
metadata.crsPlaceholderDetected: Leave blank to use detected CRS ({{crs}})
metadata.crsHelpText: Override the detected CRS (EPSG code). Leave empty to use detected value.
metadata.temporalHelpText: Date range for this dataset. Used for temporal search.
metadata.compressionHelp: COG output compression. DEFLATE is default. ZSTD for speed, JPEG/WEBP for imagery.
metadata.resamplingHelp: Method for overview generation and reprojection. Auto selects based on data type.
metadata.nodataHelp: Override or assign a nodata value for this raster.
metadata.embeddedGeometryHelp: This source already includes native geometry, so GeoLens will keep it during import.

bulk.readyDescription: Open the finished datasets directly, or keep uploading while the rest of the jobs settle.
